summary: Proposal is partially obsolete — CI pipeline, Dockerfile, and .dockerignore already exist. Requires major revision to reflect current codebase state. Remaining value is CD pipeline, Dependabot, CODEOWNERS, and deployment scripts (~40% of original scope).

findings.description: Proposal marks Dockerfile, .dockerignore, and .github/workflows/ci.yml as NEW, but all three already exist in the repository. The Dockerfile is a multi-stage build with Python 3.12, uv dependency management, and Braintrust extra. ci.yml has lint, test, contract-test, and validate-profiles jobs.
findings.resolution: Remove tasks 1.1-1.4 (Docker config), 2.1-2.6 (CI workflow creation). Replace with tasks to audit/improve existing files. Focus remaining effort on deploy.yml, dependabot.yml, CODEOWNERS, and deployment scripts.

findings.description: Proposal specifies Redis service container (redis:7) in CI and mentions Redis in docker-compose. Redis was completely removed from the project — no application code uses it (removed from docker-compose, settings, profiles, CI, pyproject.toml).
findings.resolution: Remove all Redis references from proposal.md, design.md, and tasks.md. Remove REDIS_URL from secrets table.

findings.description: Proposal specifies postgres:15 image but the project uses pgvector/pgvector:pg17. Both docker-compose.yml and ci.yml already use pg17. Using postgres:15 would miss pgvector extension and cause test failures for search/embedding tests.
findings.resolution: Update all PostgreSQL references from postgres:15 to pgvector/pgvector:pg17 to match existing configuration.

findings.description: Proposal specifies python:3.11-slim in Dockerfile but the project requires Python >=3.12 (pyproject.toml) and the existing Dockerfile uses python:3.12-slim.
findings.resolution: Update Dockerfile references from python:3.11-slim to python:3.12-slim.

findings.description: Design doc mentions 'uv sync --frozen --no-dev' for dependency install but the existing Dockerfile uses 'uv sync --frozen --no-dev --no-editable --extra braintrust'. Missing --extra braintrust causes silent import failure in production (documented in CLAUDE.md gotchas).
findings.resolution: Update Dockerfile examples to include --extra braintrust flag.

findings.description: Spec requires coverage threshold ('pytest SHALL pass with configured coverage threshold') but neither the spec nor design doc specifies what the threshold is. The existing ci.yml runs tests without --cov flag and has no coverage gate.
findings.resolution: Either specify a concrete coverage threshold (e.g., 70%) or remove the coverage requirement and add it as a future enhancement.

findings.description: Spec has no requirement for Alembic single-head enforcement, but the existing ci.yml already runs 'python scripts/check_alembic_heads.py' in the lint job. This is a critical quality gate (documented in CLAUDE.md) that the spec should capture.
findings.resolution: Add a spec scenario: 'GIVEN a pull request, WHEN CI runs, THEN Alembic SHALL have a single migration head.'

findings.description: No requirement for profile validation in CI. The existing ci.yml has a validate-profiles job that validates all profiles/*.yaml and checks for hardcoded secrets. This should be in the spec.
findings.resolution: Add spec scenarios for profile validation and secrets scanning in CI.

findings.description: No requirement for contract/fuzz testing in CI. The existing ci.yml has a contract-test job running Schemathesis. This is a significant quality gate not captured in the spec.
findings.resolution: Add spec scenario for API contract testing via Schemathesis.

findings.description: Design proposes 'docker-compose pull && up' deployment strategy (Decision 6) but the project deploys to Railway (see docs/MOBILE_DEPLOYMENT.md, skills/aca-deployment, railway.yaml profile). The deployment target is Railway, not a docker-compose-managed server.
findings.resolution: Revise deployment strategy to align with Railway deployment model. The CD pipeline should trigger Railway deployment via Railway CLI or API, not docker-compose. Reference existing aca-deployment skill and Railway deployment docs.

findings.description: Proposal lists GHCR_TOKEN for pushing to GitHub Container Registry, but the existing Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml suggest the production image is built and deployed by Railway directly (Railway builds from source). GHCR may not be needed if Railway handles the build.
findings.resolution: Clarify whether GHCR is needed alongside Railway's built-in builder. If Railway builds from source, the CD pipeline only needs to trigger a Railway deployment, not push to GHCR.

findings.description: Task 4.4 lists NEO4J credentials as environment secrets but Neo4j credentials are already managed via profile-based configuration (.secrets.yaml + profiles). Adding them as GitHub Environment secrets creates a second source of truth.
findings.resolution: Document that secrets are managed via Railway environment variables (for production) and .secrets.yaml (for development). GitHub Environment secrets should only store values that the CD pipeline itself needs (e.g., RAILWAY_TOKEN).

findings.description: Spec requires image tagging with commit SHA and 'latest' but has no requirement for semantic version tags. Railway may need specific tags or branches for deployment triggers.
findings.resolution: Consider adding requirement for branch-based or semver tags if Railway deployment is trigger-based.

findings.description: Task 5.3 proposes adding Opik service to docker-compose.yml, but Opik already has its own docker-compose.opik.yml (managed via 'make opik-up'). Merging into the main file would force all developers to run Opik.
findings.resolution: Keep Opik in its separate compose file. This task is already done and should be removed.

findings.description: Tasks are numbered 1-10 but count to 49 items total. The numbering uses section.item format (1.1, 1.2...) which is clear but some sections have very different granularity (Section 1 has 4 tasks, Section 2 has 8).
findings.resolution: After removing completed/obsolete tasks, re-balance remaining sections for consistent granularity.
